args.content:
From: cto@example.com
Subject: Project Alpha Kickoff

We are kicking off Project Alpha, a customer-facing analytics dashboard that will replace the legacy reporting system. The team is 6 FTEs across four months with $220K in blended spend. The confirmed stack is PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB for the data layer, FastAPI for the API, React + Recharts for the frontend, and OAuth2 SSO for authentication. Kafka and Flink will handle the streaming pipeline. Approved budget is $340K split across infrastructure, engineering, and QA.

From: pm@example.com
Subject: Project Alpha Timeline

The Project Alpha delivery schedule is as follows: Phase 1 Jan 20 - Feb 14 (data pipeline and schema), Phase 2 Feb 17 - Mar 14 (API layer and integration), Phase 3 Mar 17 - Apr 18 (frontend and QA), beta Apr 21, GA May 12. Please keep the Project Alpha timeline visible to stakeholders and flag any slippage immediately.

From: security@example.com
Subject: Project Alpha Security Review Results

The security review for Project Alpha identified the following issues. Cross-tenant isolation gaps exist in the multi-tenant query engine. SSRF risk is present in the report generation endpoint. WebSocket connections must implement per-message authentication. Missing audit logging for admin operations across all tenants. A separate WebSocket gateway adds ~2 weeks to the delivery estimate. Shipping secure is non-negotiable, so the timeline has been adjusted accordingly.

From: finance@example.com
Subject: Project Alpha Budget Update

The Project Alpha budget has been expanded from $340K to $410K due to additional Kafka scaling requirements. Finance also modeled an interim scenario where higher infrastructure assumptions could push the total to $432K before cost optimizations were approved. Phase 1 actual spend came in at $78K versus the original $85K infrastructure plan, which is positive. The revised Kafka cluster sizing to 6 brokers instead of 3 adds an additional $23K/month in infrastructure costs.

From: sales@example.com
Subject: Project Alpha Sales Pipeline

Customer demand for Project Alpha is strong. Confirmed prospects include Acme Corp ($500K ARR potential), GlobalTech ($350K ARR), Summit Financial ($420K ARR), Nexus Industries ($280K ARR), and DataFlow Inc ($300K ARR). Those five prospects represent $1.85M ARR. The broader Project Alpha pipeline is now at $2.8M versus the earlier $2.1M projection. Customers are asking for custom alerting, anomaly detection, PDF export, CSV export, and API access.

From: ops@example.com
Subject: Project Alpha Operations Update

During Phase 1 of Project Alpha, the team experienced a 4-hour outage due to a Kafka rebalancing storm triggered by broker misconfiguration. Data volume is now projected at 2TB/day. The updated timeline for Project Alpha moves beta to May 6 and GA to May 27 after the security work and WebSocket gateway plan added delay. Phase 2 now ends Apr 1 and Phase 3 ends May 3 to accommodate the security remediations. The delay was framed as a deliberate tradeoff to ship the product securely.

params.prompt: Review all emails in the emails/ folder and create a comprehensive Project Alpha summary saved to alpha_summary.md, covering the key decisions, risks, and milestones for Project Alpha.
